title: Clank & Slop - The Tape
tagline: - READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE -

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: AI memory listed in New York. SK Hynix priced its Nasdaq debut at $149 and raised about $26.5bn, the largest US listing ever by a foreign company, on a book run seven times over. The first close lands after this bell, so the debut's verdict is still open, but the size alone prices the compute cycle.

The strait is open on paper and thin on the water. Hormuz traffic had recovered only to about 40 tankers a day, a third of the pre-war 120-140, before 7 July missiles hit three ships and Washington reimposed oil sanctions. Brent holds its war premium while the daily transit count does the real talking.

Robots are on payroll, and the memory bill lands upstream. Humanoids logged real shifts this week at Haneda, on BMW's line, and across a 100MW solar build, while Bank of America sees shipments climbing from ~90,000 this year toward 1.2 million by 2030, demand that runs straight through the chips SK Hynix just listed.

props.caption: Forty sailings a day is a strait running at a third throttle; the barrel trades the ceiling tick by tick

items.why: A book seven times oversubscribed usually supports a first-day hold, but the Seoul underlying had fallen ~30% from June highs, which can cap the pop.
Watch whether the RSF turns ten days of drone strikes into a ground offensive, or whether aid corridors and a pause pull the city back from the El Fasher script.
Back toward triple digits and the Islamabad accord is holding; down toward the war's two-a-day floor and the strait is closing by attrition.
The last two quarter-finals set the semi field opposite France, with the Dallas semi on 14 July.
SKHYV converts to SKHY and leveraged funds SKUU and SKDD are due, a read on how much the market wants to trade the name, not just hold it.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-10 · Foreman, Sprockett, Graves, Tinkerton, Cogsworth · FIFA review call settled today (miss)

open_calls.question: SK Hynix ADRs (SKHY) close their first Nasdaq session at or above the $149 offer price
open_calls.detail: The book ran more than seven times oversubscribed, which usually supports a first-day hold; the offset is a roughly 30 percent drawdown in the Seoul-listed underlying that forced the raise down and could cap the debut.

open_calls.question: Credible reporting confirms an RSF ground assault on El Obeid, beyond the current siege and drone campaign
open_calls.detail: The encirclement, infrastructure strikes and collapsing aid match the El Fasher sequence, but the RSF has an incentive to avoid a second documented genocide finding, and sieges have ended in negotiated withdrawals before.

open_calls.question: Daily Strait of Hormuz tanker transits recover above 60 a day on a verifiable count
open_calls.detail: Traffic had climbed only to about 40 a day before the 7 July attacks capped the recovery; a move above 60 needs a clear stretch without a fresh strike and confidence that the accord holds.

open_calls.question: AFA or a senior Argentina player explicitly ties the Egypt comeback to Independence Day in an official channel
open_calls.detail: The public square already did the symbolic work, and the official holiday readouts reviewed to date did not bind the match to the ceremony; the window closes with the quarter-final.

open_calls.question: France or Syria announces an additional security or reconstruction mechanism tied to Macron's Damascus visit
open_calls.detail: The visit carried reconstruction language and the blasts made security visible, but the probability stays below even until an official mechanism, not a statement, appears.

items.what: SK Hynix priced 177.9m ADSs at $149 to raise about $26.5bn, trading as SKHYV before converting to SKHY on 13 July, when leveraged ETFs SKUU and SKDD are also due to launch.
Brent held its war premium near $77 as Hormuz tanker traffic stayed a third of pre-war levels; the US reimposed the Iran oil sanctions it had lifted under the Islamabad accord.
SK Hynix holds the majority of high-bandwidth-memory revenue, the chips beside AI accelerators in the data centres of the largest US cloud buyers, the demand its record listing is priced against.
